opening.title: Music that feels your focus.
opening.subtitle: FlowDJ generates adaptive music in real-time using Web Audio API synthesis, biofeedback loops, and zero dependencies. No backend. No signup. Just open it.

what-it-is.title: A single HTML file that reads your brain state through your fingers.
what-it-is.body: FlowDJ is ~2500 lines of pure HTML/CSS/JS. No build step, no npm install, no framework. It synthesizes music using FM synthesis and adapts in real-time based on two signals:
bullets: Keystroke dynamics — typing cadence, rhythm, pauses → mapped to a flow score (0-100)
Microphone RMS — ambient sound levels feed back into the music engine
Focus analysis — rolling 80-word window analyzes keyword density vs drift words

branch-interest.title: What draws you in?
branch-interest.subtitle: Each system is interesting on its own. Pick what you want to explore.

branches.label: The audio synthesis
branches.desc: FM synthesis, 6 vibes, delay, LFO, swing, sub bass — all in the browser

branches.label: The flow tracking
branches.desc: How keystrokes become a real-time cognitive state signal

branches.label: The Spotify integration
branches.desc: How it bridges generated music with your real library

audio-deep.title: Browser-native FM synthesis with personality.
audio-deep.body: Six distinct vibes — chill, focus, energy, dreamy, dark, jazz — each with unique oscillator configurations, harmonic ratios, and envelope shapes. The engine layers:
bullets: FM carrier + modulator — frequency modulation gives each note harmonic richness without samples
Sub bass oscillator — separate low-frequency layer for warmth
Delay with feedback — stereo ping-pong delay creates spatial depth
LFO modulation — slow oscillation on filter cutoff and amplitude for movement
Swing timing — humanized note placement, not rigid grid quantization

flow-deep.title: Keystrokes as cognitive telemetry.
flow-deep.body: Every keypress feeds a flow scoring algorithm. The system tracks inter-key intervals, burst patterns, and pause durations to estimate cognitive state across four zones:
bullets: Idle (0-20) — no activity or very sparse input. Music stays ambient, low energy.
Warming (20-50) — sporadic typing, getting started. Music gradually introduces melodic elements.
Flow (50-80) — sustained rhythmic input. Full musical expression, harmonic complexity.
Peak (80-100) — intense sustained focus. Music matches energy, minimal interruption.
flow-deep.code: // Flow zones drive music adaptation const zones = { idle: { min: 0, max: 20, energy: 0.2 }, warming: { min: 20, max: 50, energy: 0.5 }, flow: { min: 50, max: 80, energy: 0.8 }, peak: { min: 80, max: 100, energy: 1.0 } }

spotify-deep.title: Your music library as a focus anchor.
spotify-deep.body: FlowDJ connects to Spotify via the Threshold SDK's connect token system (thld_ct_). Once connected, it doesn't just play your music — it analyzes your listening patterns to build a taste profile:
bullets: Audio features extraction — energy, valence, tempo, danceability from your top tracks
Focus anchors per project — associate specific playlists/artists with projects you're working on
Taste signal push — your music preferences are pushed to the Threshold SDK as a spotify-taste signal
Seamless handoff — generated music fades into Spotify playback when you're in flow, and back when you drift

converge-architecture.title: Three systems, one feedback loop.
converge-architecture.body:
The audio engine, flow tracker, and Spotify integration aren't separate features — they're a single adaptive loop:

Input (keystrokes, mic, Spotify context) → State (flow score, zone, drift detection) → Output (music adaptation, vibe shifts, Spotify handoff) → Input (the music affects your focus, which changes your typing...).

The focus analyzer adds a text-level signal: it watches what you're writing, not just how fast you're typing. A rolling 80-word window measures keyword density vs drift words. If you start writing about lunch instead of your codebase, the music responds.

branch-explore.title: Where do you want to go from here?
branch-explore.subtitle: Pick what matters to you.

branches.label: How does it connect to Threshold?
branches.desc: The SDK integration, signal protocol, and why this is a wedge product

branches.label: I want to try it
branches.desc: How to run it, deploy it, or contribute

threshold-connection.title: FlowDJ is the first consumer of the Threshold SDK.
threshold-connection.body: Every 5 seconds, FlowDJ pushes a signal to thresholdlabs.io/api/signals/ai-dj containing your current flow state. This is Pattern 1 of the Threshold signal protocol — apps pushing structured observations about user behavior.
bullets: Signal push — flow score, zone, active project, drift state. Error contract: 401/403 disables push, 429 respects Retry-After, 503 exponential backoff
spotify-taste signal — extracted audio features pushed after Spotify auth, representing your music preferences as a portable signal
Companion bridge — companion.js polls project-control on localhost:9111, auto-detects which project you're working on, enables cross-project drift detection
Pattern 2 (upcoming) — cross-user signal reads via trust edges. Your flow data could inform team dashboards or shared music sessions

try-it.title: Zero friction to run.
try-it.body: FlowDJ is live at flowdj.pages.dev — open it and start typing. No account, no install, no config. For local development:
try-it.code: # Clone and run git clone https://github.com/Threshold-Labs/flowdj cd flowdj docker compose up # → http://localhost:8088 # Or just open the file open index.html # Deploy to Cloudflare Pages npx wrangler pages deploy . --project-name flowdj
bullets: Single HTML file — everything is inline, no build step
Docker: nginx:alpine on port 8088
Cloudflare Pages: auto-deploys on push to main

closing.title: Your attention deserves a soundtrack that listens back.
closing.subtitle: FlowDJ is open source, zero-dependency, and running right now at flowdj.pages.dev. Fork it, extend it, or just put your headphones on and start typing.
